classes.name: nrfregtool.common.Record

methods.name: nrfregtool.common.Record.update
documentation:
Overridden method. If a Record is given as the parameter and already has an entry in the
mapping, update the Record contents instead. This avoids overwriting the Record entirely
when it should instead be patched or updated as the script processes all input contents.
If no matching Record exists or the argument is not a Record object, the parent method
functionality is used.

```
param other

Positional arguments. See  for more details.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments. See  for more details.
```

methods.name: nrfregtool.common.Record.as_hex
documentation: Helper to replace int members with their value as a hex string.

A transcript of the contents of any number of registers and their fields.
A basic Record is one that is not complete with register instance information, while a complete
Record is one that also carries instance information.

Basic example: "mem_config"
Complete example: "mem_config_0"

Example Format:
record = {
    "mem_config_0": {
        "READ": "Allowed",
        "WRITE": "Allowed",
        ...
    }
    ...
}

modules.name: nrfregtool.cli
documentation: Copyright (c) 2023 Nordic Semiconductor ASA
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

functions.name: nrfregtool.cli.cli
documentation:
Generate memory-mapped binary files of peripheral register content, based on
System View Description (SVD) files and configuration files.

Use the -h or --help option with each command to see more information about
them and their individual options/arguments.

functions.name: nrfregtool.cli.generate
documentation:
Generate binary and/or TOML file(s) for the given peripheral from an SVD and mix of TOML or
devicetree (DTS) configurations.

The SVD file provides the foundational definition of what the peripheral looks like.
Based on this definition, the registers from the configuration will be mapped accordingly into
their relevant position in memory.
By default this peripheral contains register contents that with reset values, which are then
modified by register records contained within the given configurations.

Passing a compiled DTS file into the tool, such as the zephyr.dts build artifact from Zephyr
compilations, maps property information from devicetree for nodes to their relevant register
and bitfield in the associated peripheral.

TOML configurations are a definition of explicit registers and bitfields that contain
specific values.
Specifying more than one TOML configuration will combine the various register configurations
into a single map to be applied together.
When paired with a DTS configuration, the TOML configurations will be applied after the parsed
devicetree contents are interpreted into UICR representation.

For example, on nRF54H20:

     
    nrf-regtool generate -p UICR \
    -d _build/zephyr/zephyr.dts \
    -D nrf/dts/bindings \
    -D modules/lib/nrf-regtool/dts/bindings \
    -D modules/ic-next/dts/bindings \
    -D zephyr/dts/bindings \
    -s modules/hal/nordic_haltium/nrfx/mdk/nrf54h20_application.svd \
    -o uicr.hex

Or for a TOML configuration:

     
    nrf-regtool generate -p UICR \
    -i uicr.toml \
    -s modules/hal/nordic_haltium/nrfx/mdk/nrf54h20_application.svd \
    -o uicr.hex

This command accepts multiple input and output files by repeating the related option with a
different path. This can be useful for combining multiple configuration files together into
a single output unit, which can be in the form of a hex file or TOML configuration of the
combined inputs.

For example:

     
    nrf-regtool generate -p UICR \
    -d _build/zephyr/zephyr.dts \
    -D nrf/dts/bindings \
    -D modules/lib/nrf-regtool/dts/bindings \
    -D modules/ic-next/dts/bindings \
    -D zephyr/dts/bindings \
    -s modules/hal/nordic_haltium/nrfx/mdk/nrf54h20_application.svd \
    -i register_overrides.toml \
    -o uicr.hex -o uicr_records.toml

functions.name: nrfregtool.cli.uicr_compile
documentation:
User Information Configuration Registers (UICR), in the context of certain
Nordic SoCs, are used by Secure Domain Firmware (SDFW) to configure system
resources, like memory and peripherals. When used in singular, a UICR refers
to a complete set of registers for one domain.

This command compiles a UICR binary blob from devicetree input, which should
indicate the system resource configuration required for a single domain.

For example, on nRF54H20:

     
    PYTHONPATH=zephyr/scripts/dts/python-devicetree/src \
    nrf-regtool uicr-compile \
    -e _build/zephyr/edt.pickle \
    -o uicr.hex \
    -P NRF54H20

This command is intended for use as part of the Zephyr RTOS application
development workflow. nrf-regtool depends on edtlib, a devicetree parsing
library provided by the Zephyr Project, and the input file would usually be
the edt.pickle artifact from a Zephyr build system. Using the pickle input
requires a matching revision of edtlib from the Zephyr repository.

Custom parameter type for hexadecimal integers. This allows click to automatically verify
hexadecimal parameters when given from the commandline.

modules.name: nrfregtool.parsed_dt
documentation:
This module provides helper functions and wrappers around edtlib devicetree objects that emphasize
aspects that are relevant in a product context, such as memory region and peripheral ownership.
Copyright (c) 2022 Nordic Semiconductor ASA
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

functions.name: nrfregtool.parsed_dt.dt_processor_info
documentation:
Get processor information from a domain's devicetree.

```
param devicetree

Devicetree

return

Tuple of processor information of the devicetree's domain
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.parsed_dt.sort_configs_by_type
documentation:
Sort configs by type, then group by type and sort each group internally.
This ensures that the order of the configs in the output record is consistent.

Property name

param default

Default value to return if the property does not exist

return

```

methods.name: nrfregtool.parsed_dt.ParsedDTNode.secure
documentation:
Security attribute of the node, determined solely by its bus. In Trustzone-enabled systems,
this is determined by the security bit in the node's absolute address.

This attribute does not represent any other context of security that could relate to the
node, such as properties denoting secure permissions, memory, etc.

classes.name: nrfregtool.parsed_dt.ExtractedConfig

methods.name: nrfregtool.parsed_dt.ExtractedConfig.indexed_by
documentation:
If the configuration is meant for an array register, return metadata describing how to
assign an array index to the  configuration.
If None, the configuration is not meant for an array register and does not require an index.

methods.name: nrfregtool.parsed_dt.ExtractedConfig.to_record
documentation:
Convert object to a register Record representation.
The return value contains both the Record and the number of register slots taken up by the
Record. The number of slots can be used to assign the Record to the correct register index.

```
param index

Index of the register to assign the Record to.

param kwargs

Unspecified keyword arguments to use in constructing the Record.

return

A tuple of the Record and the number of registers represented in the Record.
```
Base class for extracted configuration objects.

modules.name: nrfregtool.core
documentation: Copyright (c) 2022 Nordic Semiconductor ASA
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

functions.name: nrfregtool.core.split_register_path
documentation: Split a register path such as 'register_name.field_name' into its components

functions.name: nrfregtool.core._make_record_to_register_map
documentation:
Make a map from record name to register based on the registers in the given peripheral.
It is assumed that each register name maps to a unique record name in the peripheral.

functions.name: nrfregtool.core._find_peripheral
documentation:
Find a peripheral with the given name in the device.
Certain common prefixes and suffixes used for peripheral names can be omitted from the name.

functions.name: nrfregtool.core._strip_prefixes_suffixes
documentation:
Remove a prefix and suffix from the given string.
Up to one prefix and/or suffix is removed - if multiple of the provided strings match then
the first found is removed.

```
param word

String to strip prefixes and suffixes from.

param prefixes

List of prefixes to strip.

param suffixes

List of suffixes to strip.

return

String where prefixes and suffixes have been removed.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.core.parse_toml
documentation:
Parse register records extracted from one or more TOML configurations.
Later configurations override earlier ones.

```
param tomls

Sequence of TOML configuration files with records to load

return

Records of register content from TOML configuration files.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.core.field_as_toml
documentation:
TOML representation of a register bitfield.

```
kwarg comments

Append comment strings to the field assignment output

return

TOML string representation of the field contents.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.core.register_as_toml
documentation:
TOML representation of a peripheral register.

```
kwarg reset_values

Include unmodified Fields as their reset value

kwarg comments

Allow field comment strings in the TOML

kwarg force_32_bit_fields

List of Fields that should be given as 32-bit integers, regardless
of bit width (for example, addresses).

return

TOML string representation of the register contents.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.core.generate_toml
documentation:
Write the peripheral representation to a TOML file.

This is written without the use of 3rd party libraries, for three reasons:
: 1. Most TOML libraries focus on reading, rather than writing.
  2. The libraries that do support writing, do not always handle comments.
  3. Writing TOML is simple.

```
param out_path

Path to write TOML file to.

param peripheral

Peripheral to map register content into TOML

kwarg reset_values

Include register bit reset values along with modified values.

kwarg comments

Include register field comments in the output configuration
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.core.generate_hex
documentation:
Write the peripheral representation to a HEX file.

```
param out_path

Path to write HEX file to.

param peripheral

Peripheral to map register content into hex

kwarg reset_values

Include register bit reset values along with modified values.

kwarg fill_byte

Byte value used to fill unused memory in the peripheral space.

kwarg fill

Method for writing fill byte to memory.
```

classes.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral.__init__
documentation:
---
param base_address: Base address of the peripheral
param device: SVD device element
param mem_map: The memory map containing register values
param name: Name of the peripheral.
param record: Peripheral register record containing register values
param unconstrained: List of register paths that should not be constrained
---

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral._unconstrain
documentation:
Remove restrictions put on a value held by the register.

```
param unconstrained

List of register paths that should not be constrained

raises ValueError

If a register is not found in the peripheral set
```

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral._fill_from_record
documentation:
Fill the values of the peripheral set based on the contents of an instance of Record.

```
param record

The record that register values are filled from

raises ValueError

If the register cannot be found in the peripheral set
```

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral._fill_from_mem_map
documentation:
Fill the values of the peripheral set based on the contents of an instance of MemoryMap.

```
param mem_map

The memory map that register values are filled from

raises ValueError

If the register cannot be found in the peripheral set
```

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral.name
documentation: Name of the peripheral set.

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral.address_to_register
documentation: Map of all registers in the peripheral set, keyed by address.

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral.record_name_to_register
documentation: Map from record name to register instance.

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral.path_to_record_name
documentation: Map of register path to record name.

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral.memory_map
documentation:
Return a combined memory map of the peripherals in the set.
The returned map has a granularity of 1 byte.

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral.written_memory_map
documentation:
Return a combined memory map of the peripherals in the set, but only containing those
entries that were explicitly written to.
The returned map has a granularity of 1 byte.

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.LogicalPeripheral.address_ranges
documentation: Return a list of address ranges covered by each peripheral in the peripheral set.
Abstraction for a set of peripherals that are logically grouped together.

classes.name: nrfregtool.core.MemoryMap

methods.name: nrfregtool.core.MemoryMap.__init__
documentation:
---
param byteorder: Byte order to use when retrieving a value from an address range.
param hex_files: An iterable of hex files paths used to fill the memory map.
raises ValueError: If the 'byteorder' is unsupported.
---
The representation of a memory.

Check that the value does not change when the lower bits are truncated, and raise
an exception if it does.

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.from_dts
documentation:
Gather information from parsed devicetree nodes on configurations that must set in UICR
in order to be allocated on boot, then map those records into a memory map of UICR
contents.

```
param devicetree

Devicetree structure.

param product

Product name.

return

Meta-record of extracted UICR records from devicetree nodes.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.extract_all_configs
documentation: Extract all UICR configurations.

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.convert_configs_to_records
documentation:
Convert a list of extracted configurations to a list of UICR peripheral records.
The records are sorted and indexed automatically.

```
param uicr

Interpreted UICR.

param configs

List of extracted configurations.

return

List of UICR peripheral records.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.extract_vtor_configs
documentation:
Extract INITSVTOR and INITNSVTOR record information from parsed devicetree nodes.

```
param chosen

Devicetree node containing chosen properties

return

List of VTOR records for use with UICR.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr._memory_configs_from_node
documentation:
Make a map from owner ID to memory configuration based on a devicetree node with owned memory
compatible. The node's "nordic,access" property may reference multiple, unique owner IDs.

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.extract_memory_configs
documentation:
Extract memory configuration record information from a list of parsed devicetree nodes.

```
param nodes

Parsed devicetree nodes

param domain_id

Domain ID associated to the UICR processor.

return

List of memory records for use with UICR.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr._processor_id_from_node
documentation:
Determine the processor ID of the CPU that will receive IRQs for a given peripheral.
This is mapped from the interrupt controller associated with the peripheral node.

```
param node

Parsed devicetree node

param default

Processor ID of the domain CPU, which is used by default
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.extract_periph_configs
documentation:
Extract peripheral config record information from a list of parsed devicetree nodes. If the
peripheral contains GPIO pin properties, records for these are extracted from those properties
while parsing the node.

```
note

This is only relevant for global peripheral nodes, and should not be used for locals.

param nodes

Parsed devicetree nodes

param processor

Information related to the domain processor.

return

List of peripheral records for use with UICR.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr._is_gpios_prop
documentation: Returns True if prop is a standard property referencing gpio pins

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr._has_nordic_gpio_controller
documentation: Returns True if gpios_entry refers to a nordic GPIO node

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr._gpio_pin_security_from_node
documentation: Get the security level of GPIO pin based on the security of the node using it

an entry in a 'gpios' property

Product name

'gpios' property entries

Pin Control objects

Product name

Parsed devicetree nodes

'gpios' property entries

Pin Control objects

Additional pins to insert into the record.

Parsed devicetree nodes

Parsed devicetree nodes

Parsed devicetree nodes

List of GPIOTE configuration records for use with UICR.

Parsed devicetree nodes

Configure for a Trustzone secure system

List of GRTC configuration records for use with UICR.

Extract DPPIC record information from a list of parsed devicetree nodes.

Parsed devicetree nodes

Configure for a Trustzone secure system

return

List of DPPIC configuration records for use with UICR.

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.extract_ipcmap_configs
documentation:
Extract IPCMAP record information from a list of IPCT nodes and returns a list of records
to use with the other UICR resource records. Due to limitations of devicetree, this function
needs to know what a "unit" of an IPC mapping looks like in the property, in other words a
subset of an integer array comprising of the relevant information. See the description of the
related IPCT properties for more information.

```
param nodes

List of parsed IPCT nodes from devicetree

return

List of IPCMAP records extracted from the source/sink mappings of the IPCT nodes.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.extract_ipct_configs
documentation:
Extract IPCT channel record information from a list of parsed devicetree nodes.

```
param nodes

Parsed devicetree nodes

param secure

Configure for a Trustzone secure system

return

Tuple of parsed of IPCT configuration records and IPCMAP records for use with UICR.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr.extract_trace_configs
documentation:
Extract TRACE record information from a list of parsed devicetree nodes.

```
param nodes

Parsed devicetree nodes

return

List of either none or one TraceConfig record for use with UICR.
```

IPC node

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr._mbox_owner_ids_get

param mbox_nodes

Dictionary of mbox nodes

return

remote Owner ID.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr._shmem_is_secure
documentation:
Check if a memory node is secure. That is, it has an ancestor
with owned memory compatible and secure-only permission for all given owners.
It stops at the first node with compatible owned memory.

```
param shmem_node

Node to check security attribute of.

return

True if node is secure, otherwise False.
```

functions.name: nrfregtool.uicr._log_extracted_uicr_configs
documentation:
Dump all UICR/EXTUICR configurations extracted from devicetree that were appended to the global
UICR logging map.

This function and its internals allow greater flexibility in the order and output styling of
user-friendly info dumps for UICR contents (such as associating IPCMAPs with their actual IPCTs
in the IPCT logging section, when their UICR configurations are independent), as well as
reducing the clutter of logging formats and calls in the associated extraction functions.

classes.name: nrfregtool.uicr.ResourceCompatible
documentation:
Enumeration of devicetree compatibles that are of relevant to nodes containing information from
UICR. These can be base compatible substring that are not version specific in order to allow
for more fuzzy checking of relevant nodes, preventing breakage from naming convention changes
in the external repository that defines them.
